action.reasoning: Observation: diagnostics show checkpoints/best.pt likely uses the original vision_model.* key format with a 1024-d CLIP-L classifier, while current model.py expects clip.vision_model.* and caused continuation load failure. Analysis: the best submitted checkpoint is still strong, but current code must be made compatible before any final submission. A conservative continuation from the known-good checkpoint can only help if validation improves, and will save the initial checkpoint first so the submitted checkpoint cannot be worse by val selection. Decision: patch model.py/predict.py to support the original vision-only key format and flexible checkpoint loading, rewrite train_continue.py with robust checkpoint key normalization, freeze most CLIP layers, fine-tune only the last 8 vision layers plus head for 3 epochs with small LR, then submit the selected checkpoint.
